explanation: Bypasses the rule by using decimal notation (e.g., [char]65) instead of hexadecimal notation ([char]0x41). The Sigma rule strictly looks for the '0x' string.
Bypasses the rule by inserting a whitespace character between '[char]' and '0x'. The rule relies on a literal string match of '[char]0x' without accounting for spacing.
Bypasses the rule by utilizing the .NET [Convert]::ToChar() method to perform the character casting, thereby completely avoiding the specific syntax targeted by the detection.
Bypasses the rule by utilizing the fully qualified .NET class name [System.Char] instead of the [char] type accelerator, evading the exact '[char]0x' substring match.
Bypasses the rule by inserting a single whitespace character between the type accelerator and the hex value, which breaks the contiguous '[char]0x' pattern the rule expects.
Bypasses the rule by supplying the character code in decimal format (65) rather than hexadecimal (0x41), entirely avoiding the '0x' string component.
Bypasses the rule by using the .NET Convert class method to cast the hexadecimal value to a character, avoiding the '[char]' cast operator syntax altogether.
